title: hibiscus moscheutos subsp. palustris
intro: Hibiscus moscheutos subsp. palustris, also known as the marsh rose-mallow, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the hibiscus family and is native to the eastern regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its rich content of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of marsh rose-mallow include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The herbal preparations of hibiscus moscheutos subsp. palustris include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
